question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCallRequest with gas <= 10000 so that scaleGas() returns 0 tokens consumed from gasLimitBucket, effectively bypassing the gas-per-second throttle entirely and flooding the mirror node with unlimited contract calls, causing missing or incorrect records due to resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of requests each with gas=10000 (the GAS_SCALE_FACTOR boundary) so that scaleGas() returns exactly 0 for every request, consuming zero tokens from gasLimitBucket and allowing unlimited throughput, causing the mirror node to export incorrect or missing records due to overload?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket is not created with SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED (unlike gasLimitBucket) to race concurrent requests past the per-second rate check, causing more requests than intended to be processed and leading to incorrect records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests where gas is exactly 10001 so that scaleGas() returns floor(10001/10000)=1 token, consuming only 1 token per request regardless of actual gas, allowing a user to submit requests with near-zero gas cost to the throttle and causing the mirror node to export records with incorrect gas accounting?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated InvalidParametersException paths in ContractController.call() to cause throttleManager.restore(request.getGas()) to be called repeatedly, over-filling the gasLimitBucket beyond its configured capacity and allowing subsequent requests to bypass the gas throttle, leading to missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest that passes throttle() but then triggers InvalidParametersException in validateContractMaxGasLimit(), causing restore() to be called with the full gas value and returning tokens to gasLimitBucket that were already consumed, effectively allowing a second request to bypass the gas throttle and causing incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential check order in throttle() — rateLimitBucket checked before gasLimitBucket — by sending a burst of requests that exhaust rateLimitBucket for legitimate users while the attacker's own requests consume gasLimitBucket tokens, causing legitimate contract call records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with the maximum allowed gas value (e.g., 15000000) to rapidly drain the gasLimitBucket, causing all subsequent legitimate requests from other users to receive ThrottleException with GAS_PER_SECOND_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when gasLimitBucket.tryConsume() fails after rateLimitBucket.tryConsume() succeeds, the consumed rate-limit token is not restored, causing the rateLimitBucket to be permanently depleted by failed gas-throttle requests and blocking legitimate users from having their records exported?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: action()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest whose fields (DATA, FROM, TO, BLOCK) match a configured REJECT filter, causing a ThrottleException('Invalid request') to be thrown without any gas token restoration, permanently consuming gasLimitBucket tokens and starving legitimate requests whose records would otherwise be exported?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: action()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest that matches a THROTTLE filter whose per-filter bucket is exhausted, causing a ThrottleException without restoring the already-consumed gasLimitBucket tokens, leading to a gas token leak that causes legitimate records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that multiple requestFilter entries are evaluated in sequence and each matching filter calls action() independently, so a request matching multiple THROTTLE filters consumes multiple per-filter bucket tokens in one call, causing the per-filter buckets to drain faster than intended and blocking legitimate records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that match a LOG-action filter, causing log.info('{}', request) to be called with the full ContractCallRequest object including sensitive fields (FROM address, DATA payload), and if logging is synchronous, cause a logging bottleneck that delays record processing and results in missing or delayed records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the probabilistic sampling in RequestProperties.test() where action != THROTTLE uses RandomUtils.secure().randomLong(0L, 100L) >= rate, meaning at rate=99 approximately 1% of matching requests bypass the LOG/REJECT action, allowing crafted requests to inconsistently evade REJECT filters and produce unexpected records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the THROTTLE action in RequestProperties.test() skips the RandomUtils sampling check (action != ActionType.THROTTLE is false), meaning THROTTLE-filtered requests are always evaluated deterministically while LOG/REJECT are probabilistic, creating an inconsistency that an attacker can use to predict which requests will be rejected and time submissions to avoid REJECT filters?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with gas=0 that passes scaleGas() returning 0 (consuming no gasLimitBucket tokens), then trigger an InvalidParametersException to cause restore(0) to be called, which is a no-op, but the rateLimitBucket token was consumed and not restored, causing a net rate-limit token loss that blocks legitimate records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that opcodeRateLimitBucket is not synchronized (no SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED) to race concurrent opcode requests past throttleOpcodeRequest(), causing more opcode trace records than intended to be exported to the mirror node, corrupting the record set?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the extremely low default opcodeRequestsPerSecond=1 combined with the lack of per-IP throttling to send a single opcode request per second from many different source IPs, collectively overwhelming the opcode endpoint and causing opcode trace records to be missing or incorrect on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of an upper bound check in restore() — gasLimitBucket.addTokens(tokens) is called without checking if the bucket is already at capacity — to over-fill the gasLimitBucket beyond its configured gasPerSecond limit, allowing more gas than intended to be processed per second and causing incorrect records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send a ContractCallRequest with a negative gas value (if not validated upstream) so that scaleGas() computes Math.floorDiv(negative, 10000) returning a negative number, causing gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(negative) to behave unexpectedly (bucket4j may treat negative consume as always-true), bypassing the gas throttle entirely and causing missing records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the request filter list throttleProperties.getRequest() is iterated without short-circuiting after the first REJECT action, meaning if multiple REJECT filters match, multiple ThrottleExceptions would be thrown sequentially (only the first is thrown), but gasLimitBucket tokens are consumed once and never restored, causing token leakage and missing records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: action()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest whose DATA field contains a very long hex string that matches a CONTAINS filter, causing log.info('{}', request) to log an extremely large payload, filling disk or log buffers and causing the mirror node to drop or corrupt subsequent records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that rateLimitBucket uses refillGreedy strategy, meaning tokens refill continuously rather than in discrete windows, to time requests precisely at refill boundaries and submit more requests per second than the nominal limit, causing more records than intended to be exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the greedy refill strategy of gasLimitBucket to time high-gas requests at the exact moment the bucket refills, consuming the entire bucket capacity in a single burst and starving all other users' requests, causing their records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-user or per-IP throttling in ThrottleManagerImpl — all users share the same rateLimitBucket and gasLimitBucket — to monopolize the shared buckets and cause other users' contract call records to be missing from the mirror node?
